blocks.body: Present Simple — habits, facts, repeated actions, and words like always or often.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Present Simple is the tense for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with I/you/we/they + V1 and he/she/it + V-s.","example":"I work. / She works."},{"label":"Negatives and Questions","color":"blue","text":"Then add do / does for negatives and questions.","example":"They don't work. / Does he work?"},{"label":"Frequency words","color":"amber","text":"Words like always, usually, often, sometimes, never make sentences more natural.","example":"She usually walks to school."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form, then move to negatives, questions, and frequency. Each card shows the matching Present Simple table rules.","items":[{"label":"A1-A2","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"Affirmative forms, core rules, and main uses.","rules":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","formula":"V1","text":"Use the base form of the verb with no ending.","example":"I / You / We / They work. They work every day."},{"label":"He / She / It","formula":"V-s / V-es","text":"Add -s / -es to the verb.","example":"He / She / It works. She works every day."}]},{"label":"A1-A2","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"do not / does not, don't / doesn't, and the base form of the verb.","rules":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","formula":"don't + V1","text":"After don't, keep the verb in the base form.","example":"I / You / We / They don't work. We don't work on Sunday."},{"label":"He / She / It","formula":"doesn't + V1","text":"After doesn't, use the verb without -s.","example":"He / She / It doesn't work. He doesn't work on Sunday."}]},{"label":"A1-A2","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Do you...? / Does he...? and short answers.","rules":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","formula":"Do + subject + V1?","text":"Put Do at the beginning of the question.","example":"Do I / you / we / they work? Do they work here?"},{"label":"He / She / It","formula":"Does + subject + V1?","text":"Put Does first and keep the main verb without -s.","example":"Does he / she / it work? Does she work here?"},{"label":"Short answers","formula":"do / don't · does / doesn't","text":"Repeat the auxiliary verb in short answers.","example":"Yes, I do. / No, she doesn't."}]},{"label":"A1-A2","title":"Adverbs of Frequency","subtitle":"always, usually, often, sometimes, never, and their position in the sentence.","rules":[{"label":"Before main verb","formula":"always / usually / often","text":"Put frequency adverbs before the main verb.","example":"They often work late. She usually works at home."},{"label":"After be","formula":"be + adverb","text":"With be, put the frequency adverb after the verb.","example":"He is always ready."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Where we use this tense","sections":[{"label":"Habits","color":"emerald","description":"Talk about things you do regularly.","examples":[{"en":"I get up at seven.","ua":"I get up at seven."},{"en":"They play football on Fridays.","ua":"They play football on Fridays."}]},{"label":"Facts","color":"blue","description":"Use the tense for simple facts and general truths.","examples":[{"en":"Water boils at 100°C.","ua":"Water boils at 100°C."},{"en":"My sister lives in Lviv.","ua":"My sister lives in Lviv."}]},{"label":"Repeated actions","color":"amber","description":"Show actions that happen again and again at school, at home, or at work.","examples":[{"en":"He takes the bus to work.","ua":"He takes the bus to work."},{"en":"We often have lunch together.","ua":"We often have lunch together."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Present Simple = habits, facts, and repeated actions.","Use V1 with I / you / we / they.","With he / she / it, usually add -s / -es.","Use do / does for negatives and questions.","Adverbs of frequency help you say how often something happens."]}

{"title":"4. Core rules from the 4 subtopics","items":["Forms and Use: I / you / we / they + V1; he / she / it + V-s / V-es. Use it for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","Negatives: I / you / we / they + do not / don't + V1; he / she / it + does not / doesn't + V1. After does, use the verb without -s.","Questions and Short Answers: put Do / Does at the beginning: Do you work? Does she work? Answer briefly: Yes, I do. / No, she doesn't.","Adverbs of Frequency: always, usually, often, sometimes, never usually go before the main verb, but after be: She often reads. / He is always ready."]}
